projects.title: Case Studies
projects.subtitle: Products, projects, and processes I've built — and everything I learned along the way.

panificio.title: Panificio Da Sergio
panificio.subtitle: Stakeholder Delivery: E-Commerce for a Family Bakery
panificio.description: Delivered a full e-commerce platform for a traditional Italian bakery — managing stakeholder expectations, technical constraints, and a tight budget. A lesson in scope negotiation, client communication, and shipping under real-world constraints.
features: Stakeholder management: translating family needs into technical requirements
Budget-constrained delivery: full e-commerce under material constraints
End-to-end: product catalog → orders → admin dashboard

vulnclaw.title: VulnClaw
vulnclaw.subtitle: Product Delivery: AI CLI from Zero to Open Source Community
vulnclaw.description: Built an open-source AI penetration testing CLI from scratch. Managed scope, community contributions, roadmap prioritization, and shipping a v0.4.0 with active users. Key lesson: starting narrow beats building broad every time.
features: Product scoping: from port scanner to full AI agent in 6 months
Community management: triaging PRs, issues, and feature requests
Roadmap prioritization: saying "no" to good ideas to ship the essential ones
Open source as a delivery strategy

trova.title: Trova
trova.subtitle: SaaS Product: From Idea to €49 Paid Product
trova.description: Designed, built, and shipped a production-ready SaaS boilerplate (Next.js 16, Stripe, Auth, i18n) as a paid product. Managing the full delivery lifecycle: requirements, scope control, payments, documentation, and support.
features: Full product lifecycle management: concept → build → ship → support
Scope control: 12-month project compressed to 8 with ruthless prioritization
Payment integration: Stripe billing, webhooks, idempotency, failure recovery
Internationalization: i18n baked into architecture from day one
Documentation as delivery: setup guides, API refs, migration docs

steps.desc: I analyze the current state: processes, resources, KPIs, bottlenecks. In 1 week I deliver a chaos map and a prioritized action plan.
I design the operational architecture: processes, governance, metrics, automation. Everything documented, everything measurable.
I implement the system with weekly checkpoints, transparent progress, and continuous feedback. I train the team, I leave skills.
Complete documentation, knowledge transfer, success metrics. Your team operates autonomously. Optional support for evolution.

about.title: About Riccardo
about.subtitle: Operations & Delivery. PMP®. From the trenches to the control room.
about.intro: Senior Delivery Manager & Head of Operations (PMP®). 7+ years building operational architectures that scale. Enterprise portfolio €500K+, teams of 8-12, 40% faster time-to-market. Started in retail, moved through Accenture, led operations at Ciao Elsa. Now I help digital teams scale without imploding.
story: My career started in retail management — In’s Mercato, Aldi, Terranova — where I learned operational leadership, team management, and KPIs on the ground: managing shifts, costs, and store performance.

Then Accenture: enterprise application development (SAP UI5, JavaScript), process automation with measurable results: +30% efficiency, -15% administrative errors, -20% response times. The first time I understood that the right processes beat raw talent.

At Esse Solutions I managed a €500K digital project portfolio, coordinating distributed teams of 8-12 on B2B eCommerce (Magento, Shopware) and PIM/DAM (Pimcore). Introduced structured Agile: +25% productivity, -40% time-to-market, -20% completion times.

At Ciao Elsa I served as Head of Operations, building the operational architecture from the ground up for an AI-native startup. Defined processes, KPIs, team structure, and governance — from zero to functional operations in 8 weeks. A short, intense engagement that proved the playbook works even at startup speed.

Today I work as an independent consultant. I help digital companies build scalable operational architectures — processes, KPIs, governance, automation — that turn chaos into predictable execution. PMP® certified. In my free time I build tools (Trova, VulnClaw), because those who build better operations also build better products.
about.philosophy: The problem isn't a lack of talent or ideas. The problem is that operations collapse under the weight of growth. My job is to build the operational architecture that turns potential into predictable execution. I combine methodological rigor (PMP®), enterprise experience, and operational speed. No buffers. No friction. Just processes that work.
about.ctaDesc: Have a project, idea, or just want to chat? I'm always open to interesting conversations.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: This Privacy Policy explains how Riccardo Bozzato (“I”, “me”, “my”) collects, uses, and protects your personal data when you visit my website, download resources, or engage my consulting services. I take your privacy seriously — I follow GDPR and keep data collection to the minimum necessary.

sections.title: 2. Data I Collect
sections.content:
Contact form: name, email address, and message you submit voluntarily.
Freebie downloads (playbook): name and email address — used to deliver the PDF and send the email sequence (max 6 emails over 30 days).
Consulting inquiries: name, email, company, and project details you share — used to prepare proposals and deliver services.
Analytics: anonymous, aggregated page views. No cookies, no personal tracking, no fingerprinting.

sections.title: 3. How I Use Your Data
sections.content:
To respond to your inquiries and prepare proposals.
To deliver free resources (playbook PDF) and the associated email sequence.
To communicate about active consulting engagements.
To improve my website based on anonymous usage patterns.
I do not sell, rent, trade, or share your personal data with any third party for their marketing purposes.

sections.title: 4. Email Marketing (Consent & Unsubscribe)
sections.content: When you download the free playbook, you consent to receive a 6-email educational sequence over approximately 30 days. Each email includes an unsubscribe link. You can withdraw consent at any time — your request is processed immediately and you will receive no further emails. Unsubscribing does not affect communications related to active consulting engagements.

sections.title: 5. Legal Basis (GDPR)
sections.content:
I process your data based on:
Consent: when you submit a form (contact, freebie download). You can withdraw at any time.
Contractual necessity: when you engage my consulting services — data processing is required to deliver the agreed work.
Legitimate interest: responding to pre-sales inquiries and improving my services.

sections.title: 6. Data Retention
sections.content:
Contact form inquiries: deleted after 12 months of inactivity.
Freebie subscribers: retained until you unsubscribe (plus 30 days grace for re-engagement).
Consulting client data: retained for the duration of the engagement plus 24 months for tax/legal compliance (as required by Italian law).
You can request deletion of your data at any time by emailing riccardobozzato@gmail.com.

sections.title: 7. Your Rights (GDPR)
sections.content:
You have the right to:
Access your data — request a copy of everything I hold about you.
Rectify inaccurate data — ask me to correct or complete your information.
Delete your data — request erasure (subject to legal retention requirements).
Restrict processing — limit how I use your data.
Data portability — receive your data in a structured, machine-readable format.
Withdraw consent — at any time, for any processing based on consent.
To exercise any of these rights, email riccardobozzato@gmail.com. I will respond within 30 days.

sections.title: 8. Third-Party Processors
sections.content:
I use the following third-party services to operate this site and deliver services. Each has been vetted for GDPR compliance:

Resend (resend.com) — transactional email delivery and email sequence automation. Privacy: resend.com/privacy
Netlify (netlify.com) — website hosting and CDN. Privacy: netlify.com/privacy
GitHub (github.com) — source code hosting and project management. Privacy: github.com/privacy
Stripe (stripe.com) — payment processing for Trova purchases. Privacy: stripe.com/privacy
Iubenda (iubenda.com) — privacy policy generation and consent management. Privacy: iubenda.com/privacy

Data is processed within the EU and US (under SCC agreements).

sections.title: 9. Data Security
sections.content: I implement appropriate technical and organizational measures to protect your data: encrypted connections (HTTPS), access controls on databases, minimal data collection principle, and regular security reviews. No data transmission over the internet is 100% secure, but I follow industry best practices to minimize risk.

sections.title: 10. Changes to This Policy
sections.content: I may update this privacy policy from time to time. Material changes will be communicated via email (if I have your contact for active services) or via a notice on this website. The “Last updated” date at the top of this page reflects the most recent revision.

sections.title: 11. Contact
sections.content:
For any questions, requests, or concerns about this privacy policy or how I handle your data:

Riccardo Bozzato
Legnaro, PD, Italy
Email: riccardobozzato@gmail.com
Phone: +39 389 213 9542

You also have the right to lodge a complaint with the Italian Data Protection Authority (Garante per la Protezione dei Dati Personali) at garanteprivacy.it.
